grounding_note: Expansion batch: more plan-source and whose-insurer disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: The insurance company cut off my physical therapy while I still can't lift my arm above my shoulder.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the workers' comp insurer — this is my accepted claim from the warehouse injury, and they cut treatment after their one-sided 'independent' exam.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the comp insurer on my accepted warehouse-injury claim — they cut treatment after their so-called independent exam.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the paying insurer is a workers' comp carrier
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my own private health plan calling further therapy 'not medically necessary' after the car accident.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my private health plan calling more therapy unnecessary after my car accident.
scenarios.vetting_note: Comp-carrier identity routes to an entirely different remedial system.

scenarios.opening_query: My homeowner's insurance denied our claim on a technicality and we can't afford the repairs alone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || General Litigation > Mold Claims
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'technicality' is their mold exclusion — the burst pipe's real damage is mold through two floors, my son's asthma has exploded, and I'm told mold cases are their own specialty.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their 'technicality' is the mold exclusion — the pipe caused mold through two floors and my son's asthma has exploded.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether mold contamination and health effects are the core of the loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No mold — it's wind damage to the roof they're calling 'wear and tear,' and I want the coverage decision fought.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No mold — it's roof wind damage they call wear and tear.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mold-specific facts activate the dedicated label (v1's mold_vs_tenant analogue on the coverage side).

scenarios.opening_query: After my accident the other driver's insurance offered me a settlement that feels insultingly low.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Insurance (Liability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The other driver turned out to be uninsured — the lowball is from MY OWN company under my uninsured-motorist coverage, and they're treating me like an adversary.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The other driver was uninsured — the lowball is my own company on my UM coverage, treating me like the enemy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the settlement fight is first-party UM coverage
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the at-fault driver's carrier — standard third-party claim, I just think the number's wrong.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the at-fault driver's insurance — I just think their number is wrong.
scenarios.vetting_note: First-party UM disputes are coverage litigation with bad-faith exposure; users conflate the carriers.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer's insurance company is refusing to pay what they owe me and I'm done being patient.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee || Workers' Comp > State || General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the comp carrier on my back-injury claim — they owe eight weeks of time-loss checks on an accepted claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the workers' comp carrier — eight weeks of time-loss checks owed on my accepted back claim.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which employer-linked insurer and benefit is at issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the group long-term-disability plan from my benefits package — the plan administrator terminated me at the 'own occupation' switch.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the group LTD plan from my benefits — they cut me off at the own-occupation switch.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Employer's insurance' is triply ambiguous (comp/LTD/health); one question resolves it.

scenarios.opening_query: Our condo association's insurance and ours are pointing fingers while our unit stays gutted.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Real Property > Homeowners Assoc
scenarios.hidden_fact: The real blocker is the association — the CC&Rs make the HOA responsible for pipe risers, they won't produce the master policy, and their board ignores our demands.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The blocker is the association — CC&Rs put pipe risers on the HOA, they won't show the master policy, and the board ignores us.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether HOA governing-document obligations are the operative fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The HOA has cooperated fully — it's our own carrier stalling the personal-property payout for eight months.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The HOA's been fine — it's our own carrier stalling our payout for eight months.
scenarios.vetting_note: Condo water losses genuinely fork between coverage law and HOA governance.

scenarios.opening_query: Insurance is refusing to pay for the treatment that literally kept my daughter alive.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her coverage is Medicaid through the state's medically-fragile-children program — the denial is a state agency decision with its own fair-hearing process and deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's on Medicaid through the medically-fragile-children program — it's a state agency denial with a fair-hearing deadline.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the coverage is a government program with administrative appeal rights
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's our private employer family plan — the internal appeals are exhausted and external review is next.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's our private family plan — internal appeals exhausted, external review next.
scenarios.vetting_note: Program identity selects fair-hearing vs private-coverage litigation; stakes identical.
